name: Adolph Herseth
abstract: Adolph Sylvester "Bud" Herseth (July 25, 1921 – April 13, 2013) was principal trumpet in the Chicago Symphony Orchestra from 1948 until 2001, and served as principal trumpet emeritus from 2001 until his retirement in 2004.

works.t: Brandenburg Concerto No. 2 in F Major, BWV 1047

about_quotes.speaker: Wayne Bergeron
about_quotes.evidence: what did Adolph Herseth call him — the greatest brass specimen of all time. You know, because he could just do things besides his high notes. He could just do things that were easy to him. And obviously he was very inspiring to a lot of us trumpet player you know.
about_quotes.doc_title: Wayne Bergeron interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 29, 2020
